text: AI is changing the workplace in significant ways. Many routine tasks, such as data entry and scheduling, can now be automated, saving time and reducing human error. Productivity tools powered by AI help employees analyse large amounts of information quickly and create reports or presentations more efficiently. However, rather than replacing people entirely, AI often leads to human-AI collaboration, where workers use intelligent systems as assistants to enhance their own skills. This shift means that some jobs may change or disappear, but new roles are also emerging in areas like AI training and system maintenance. Adapting to these changes requires learning new digital skills.
